answer:
The quantum efficiency of 0.1 means each photon has a 10% chance of being detected, so with 100 photons the average number detected is 10. This is a binomial process, so the standard deviation is \(\sqrt{100 \times 0.1 \times 0.9} = \sqrt{9} = 3\). Thus, the correct answer is an average of 10 with an rms deviation of about 3.

\boxed{B}
